evidence.page_title: A car MELTS in the sun during a heatwave of 100F in Italy - Daily Mail
evidence.title: Incredible moment a car MELTS in the sun during heatwave in Italy
evidence.description: John Westbrook said he was astonished when he saw the Renault Megane dripping as it was parked opposite a beach in the coastal town of Caorle in northern Italy, as temperatures hit 37C.
evidence.text:
Incredible moment a car MELTED in the sun during 100F heatwave in Italy
- John Westbrook pictured damage to car caused by sheer strength of sun
- Renault Megane was parked in the blistering sunlight in Caorle for days
- The coastal town in northern Italy has been hit by temperatures of 37C
The incredible moment a car melted in the killer heatwave in Italy has been captured on camera by a British tourist.
John Westbrook said he was astonished when he saw the Renault Megane dripping as it was parked opposite a beach in the coastal town of Caorle in northern Italy, as temperatures hit 37C (99F).
The 48-year-old taxi driver from Folkestone, Kent, filmed the lights, wing mirrors and window fittings melting off the car during the heatwave, which has claimed the lives of more than 100 pensioners.
Melting: John Westbrook said he couldn't believe when he saw a Renault Megane dripping in the sweltering heat as it was parked opposite a beach in the coastal otwn of Caorle in northern Italy today
Scorching: The rubber material on the side of the car melted in the 'pure heat' of the sun, said John while the wing mirrors, lights and bumper of the navy blue Italian car were also badly damaged
He told MailOnline: 'The car was parked parallel to the beach and had been there for a few days - it was just the pure heat of the sun beating down on it that made it melt.'
'We drove past and just couldn't believe what we were seeing. Bits of the car were literally dripping off on to the road,' he added.
'Even bits of the bumper were melted and the wing mirrors were starting to buckle.
'Don't trust French cars': John took the photos while on holiday with his wife Joanne (pictured) and son Robbie
'I guess the moral of the story is don't trust French cars.'
John took the photos today while on a two week break with his wife Joanne, 45, and son Robbie, 21.
He said temperatures have hovered around the high 90s since they arrived over the weekend, forcing them to spend most of the daytime indoors.
'As soon as you walk out of the hotel it's like walking into an oven,' said John.
Killer heat: As well as damaging cars, record temperatures in north-western Italy have resulted in 140 deaths
Record temperatures in north-western Italy have left 140 pensioners dead in just the first ten days of July this year, according to data from the environmental agency there.
Tourists have been warned to take precautions against the heat, and farm animals have been given air conditioning, according to reports.

evidence.page_title: It's so hot in Italy that cars are melting - Metro
evidence.title: It's so hot in Italy that cars are melting
evidence.description: Temperatures have reached as high as 37C in the coastal town of Caorle.
evidence.text:
Remember when it was so hot in India that the road was melting?
Well now it’s so hot in Italy that cars are melting.
The sides and bumper of a navy blue Renault left out in Caorle, near Venice, deformed in the heat in the sun.
‘The car was parked parallel to the beach and been there for a few days,’ Folkestone taxi driver John Westbrook, who took the pictures, said.
MORE: Artist grows ear on arm, wants to connect it to internet. What.
‘It was just the pure heat of the sun beating down that made it melt.’
He added that the wing mirrors were ‘starting to buckle.’
Cars are designed to withstand extreme temperatures, but the Renault had been left in the sun for several days.
Temperatures have reached as high as 37C in the coastal town, and more than 100 pensioners have died.
MORE: Tooth-whitening kit leaves man with poisonous ‘sac’ in his throat
image_captions: John Westbrook said that even the wing mirrors were ‘starting to buckle’ (Picture: SWNS)

A Renault car in Caorle in Northern Italy which has melted in the intense heat of the day according to eyewitnesses, August 10 2015. John Westbrook was on holiday in the area when he came across the car which had a deformed and melted, trim, light assembly and bumper.

evidence.page_title: Can Extreme Heat Melt Car Parts Safely? - Techyenginesoil
evidence.title: Can Extreme Heat Melt Car Parts Safely? - Techyenginesoil
evidence.description: Extreme heat can damage or melt car parts. Learn the risks and simple tips to keep your vehicle safe in hot weather.
evidence.text:
Introduction
Extreme heat means very high temperatures that affect vehicles. Heat can make car parts expand, weaken, or even melt in rare cases. It puts stress on the engine, tires, and other materials.
Many drivers worry about their cars in summer. Long trips, hot roads, and strong sunlight can make the problem worse. It makes us ask: can car parts really melt under extreme heat?
In this article, we will learn how heat affects different car parts. From plastic trims to rubber tires, some materials handle heat better than others. Knowing this helps keep your car safe on the road.
How Extreme Heat Impacts Car Parts
Extreme heat can cause many problems for cars. High temperatures make metal parts expand and put pressure on the engine. Plastic and rubber parts, like trims, hoses, and tires, can soften or crack.
When a car sits in direct sunlight, the inside gets even hotter. This extra heat can damage dashboards, seats, and electronics. Over time, it makes parts weaker and less safe.
Heat also affects fluids like oil and coolant. They can thin out and lose their strength, making it harder for the engine to stay cool. That’s why cars often break down more in summer.
Why Summer Heat Puts Cars at Risk
Summer heat can be tough on cars. Hot weather makes engines work harder, especially during long drives or when stuck in traffic. The extra stress can lead to overheating.
Tires also face problems in the heat. When the road is very hot, the air inside tires expands. This increases the chance of a blowout.
Even car batteries can suffer in summer. High temperatures speed up chemical reactions inside the battery, which can shorten its life. That’s why many cars break down more often in hot months.
Key Benefits
Every car owner wants safety and long life for their vehicle. Knowing how heat affects car parts gives you the power to protect them. It also helps reduce repair costs.
Some key benefits include:
- Better car performance in hot weather
- Longer life for tires, battery, and engine
- Fewer chances of sudden breakdowns
- Peace of mind while driving in summer
Overheating the Car in Engine
When a car engine overheats, it means the temperature goes beyond safe limits. This happens when the cooling system cannot control the heat. It can damage parts inside the engine if not fixed quickly.
Overheating is common in summer or when driving long distances. Low coolant, broken fans, or hot weather can make it worse. Drivers should notice the warning signs before the engine fails.
Facts About Car and Heat in Engine
Car engines always produce heat when they run. The cooling system works hard to keep the temperature safe. If the heat goes too high, the engine can get damaged.
Extreme heat can thin the oil and put stress on parts like pistons, gaskets, and hoses. This is why cars often overheat in summer. Taking care of coolant, oil, and fans helps keep the engine safe.
Tips to Protect Car Oil from Extreme Heat
Extreme heat can make car oil thin and weak. When this happens, it cannot protect the engine properly. But with a few easy tips, you can keep your car safe in hot weather.
- Check oil levels often – Low oil heats up faster and damages the engine.
- Use the right oil grade – Thicker oil may work better in summer heat.
- Change oil on time – Old oil loses strength quickly in hot conditions.
- Keep the engine cool – Make sure coolant and fans are working well.
- Avoid long idling in heat – Running the car too long in traffic makes oil hotter.
Taking care of car oil in extreme heat helps the engine last longer. It also reduces the chances of sudden breakdowns on the road.
Final Thoughts
Extreme heat can be tough on cars, especially the engine. It can weaken oil, stress parts, and even cause sudden breakdowns.
With regular checks and small care steps, you can protect your car from heat damage. A little attention today helps your car stay safe and strong on the road.
Conclusion
Extreme heat is not just uncomfortable for people. It also creates serious problems for cars. Engines, oil, and parts can suffer when the temperature is too high.
The good news is that drivers can prevent most of these issues. Regular checks, proper oil, and a healthy cooling system make a big difference. With simple care, your car stays safe even on the hottest days.
FAQs
1. Can extreme heat really melt car parts?
Yes, very high heat can soften or weaken plastic and rubber parts, though metal is harder to melt.
2. Why does my car overheat in summer?
Hot weather puts extra stress on the engine and cooling system, making overheating more likely.
3. How can I protect my car in extreme heat?
Check coolant, use the right oil, and avoid long idling in hot weather.
4. Can heat damage car batteries?
Yes, high temperatures speed up chemical wear, which can shorten battery life.
5. What are the signs of heat damage in a car?
Warning lights, high temperature gauge, strange smells, or steam from the engine are common signs.
Noah Theo is the World Best Article Writter. Top Ranked Articles, Informative Articles, Unique Content Articles. All Articles are Written by Noah Theo..
image_captions: can extreme heat melt car parts
